sources.name: SMMT
sources.dataset: New car registrations by fuel type

metadata.methodology: EV share covers battery electric vehicles (BEVs) only, not plug-in hybrids (PHEVs). Charge points include all public charge points on the DESNZ register, including slow (3–7 kW), fast (7–22 kW), rapid (25–99 kW), and ultra-rapid (100 kW+). The 1-per-54 EVs ratio is calculated using DVLA licensed BEV stock against total public charge points. The ZEV mandate requires 22% of manufacturer sales to be zero-emission in 2024, rising to 80% by 2030 and 100% by 2035.
knownIssues: The 2030 petrol and diesel ban date may be extended to 2035 — government policy was under review as of early 2026.
Charge point density statistics mask quality differences: a slow 3 kW charger and a 150 kW ultra-rapid charger are counted equally.
EV share statistics count registrations, not stock; the total BEV fleet on UK roads was around 1.1 million in 2024, or about 4% of all cars.
